perturbation.original_text: Vendor agrees not to directly contact, for the purpose of soliciting, or selling Product directly to any customer or dealer listed in the Proprietary Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Vendor agrees not to directly contact any customer or dealer listed in the Proprietary Information.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'for the purpose of soliciting, or selling Product directly to' we create an omission, where the clause becomes more ambiguous. While the original agreement limited the non-contact clause to purposes of soliciting or selling, the modified version bans all direct contact regardless of the reason. This creates an in-text contradiction and uncertainty since the WHEREAS clauses state 'WHEREAS Vendor desires to inspect such Proprietary Information so Vendor may monitor sales through distribution;', implying that Vendor might need to contact dealers or customers for legitimate monitoring purposes. The original section limits the reason for contacting, while the new version makes it ambiguous creating a contradiction
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHEREAS Vendor desires to inspect such Proprietary Information so Vendor may monitor sales through distribution; NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual promises set out herein, the parties hereby agree as follows: 1. Except as authorized herein, Vendor agrees not to communicate, disclose, or otherwise make available all or any part of the Proprietary Information to any third party, including, but not limited to Vendor's parent, subsidiaries, or affiliated companies.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the phrase 'for the purpose of soliciting, or selling Product directly to,' creating a broader restriction on contact. The original text limited the non-contact clause to purposes of soliciting or selling, while the changed version bans all direct contact regardless of the reason. This contradicts the 'WHEREAS' clause in the Contradicted Text, which states 'WHEREAS Vendor desires to inspect such Proprietary Information so Vendor may monitor sales through distribution;' implying that Vendor might need to contact dealers or customers for legitimate monitoring purposes. This is a strong contradiction based on the explanation provided as the change creates ambiguity and uncertainty, directly conflicting with the stated purpose in the contradicted section.

perturbation.original_text: Upon termination of this Agreement by either party for any reason, Vendor shall return all Proprietary Information to Ingram within thirty (30) days, irrespective of format.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon termination of this Agreement by either party for any reason, Vendor shall return all Proprietary Information to Ingram.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'within thirty (30) days, irrespective of format' it creates an in-text contradiction because the time-frame for the return of information upon termination is unspecified. This becomes a point of ambiguity when related to section 3 that mentions about Ingram's request on returning the information. Therefore, there are now no constraints on what Ingram deems as an reasonable time frame. Therefore the section is now contradicting.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Vendor and Ingram mutually agree that all copies of the Proprietary Information and all written descriptions, extractions, or summaries thereof, whether made by Vendor or Ingram, shall be the property of Ingram, and shall, upon expiration of this Agreement or Ingram's request, be immediately returned to Ingram.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a 30-day timeframe for returning Proprietary Information. The changed text removes this timeframe, making it indefinite. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text', which states that the information 'shall... be immediately returned to Ingram' upon request. Without a timeframe, the term 'immediately' becomes ambiguous and unenforceable, creating a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Except as authorized herein, Vendor agrees not to communicate, disclose, or otherwise make available all or any part of the Proprietary Information to any third party, including, but not limited to Vendor's parent, subsidiaries, or affiliated companies.
perturbation.changed_text: Vendor agrees not to communicate, disclose, or otherwise make available all or any part of the Proprietary Information to any third party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'including, but not limited to Vendor's parent, subsidiaries, or affiliated companies' an omission is introduced. The change creates an in-text contradiction regarding the definition of a 'third party'. The original text explicitly included parent companies, subsidiaries, and affiliates, clarifying the scope of prohibited disclosures. Without this clarification, if Vendor discloses information to its parent company but argues that the parent isn't truly a 'third party' because of their corporate relationship, it creates ambiguity and a legal loophole in enforcing the non-disclosure agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except as authorized herein, Vendor agrees not to communicate, disclose, or otherwise make available all or any part of the Proprietary Information to any third party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the explicit inclusion of 'Vendor's parent, subsidiaries, or affiliated companies' from the definition of 'third party'. This omission introduces ambiguity, because the contradicted text (which is identical to the original text) states that the Vendor agrees not to disclose information to any third party. By removing the clarification in the changed text, a contradiction is created, allowing for a possible interpretation that disclosing to a parent company is not a breach of contract, thus conflicting with the original intent and creating a loophole. The perturbation type Omissions - In Text Contradiction, definition applies here. Thus, there is a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: WHEREAS Ingram has compiled and organized certain information relating to its sales which is proprietary and confidential, known as the "non-standard, subsection (i), point of sale (POS) data" component of its "Systems Sales Out Report" ("Proprietary Information"); and
perturbation.changed_text: WHEREAS Ingram has compiled and organized certain information relating to its sales which is proprietary and confidential, known as the component of its "Systems Sales Out Report" ("Proprietary Information"); and
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'non-standard, subsection (i), point of sale (POS) data' from the definition of 'Proprietary Information', it becomes ambiguous. It leaves the definition of the proprietary information to uncertainty, which makes the subsequent sections, where it refers to Proprietary Information, contradictory. For example, section 2, states 'Vendor agrees to make no more than five (5) copies of the Proprietary Information' this now creates ambiguity because it does not specify which specific pieces of information is propietary.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Vendor agrees not to use, or permit others to use, the Proprietary Information, other than for the purpose of monitoring sales through distribution. Vendor agrees to make no more than five (5) copies of the Proprietary Information unless otherwise agreed in writing between the parties; and Vendor agrees to limit distribution of and access to the Proprietary Information to those of Vendor's personnel who require access to Proprietary Information for the foregoing purpose. Vendor agrees not to directly contact, for the purpose of soliciting, or selling Product directly to any customer or dealer listed in the Proprietary Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes 'non-standard, subsection (i), point of sale (POS) data' from the definition of 'Proprietary Information'. The contradicted text states 'Vendor agrees not to use, or permit others to use, the Proprietary Information'. Because the definition of proprietary information has been altered, this creates a contradiction. The Vendor may not know what information is included under Proprietary Information, creating a direct conflict within the document.
